explanation: Subgingival overhanging margins trap plaque and favor anaerobic gram-negative periodontal pathogens; smooth or supragingival contours are less plaque-retentive.
Influenza spreads by respiratory droplets/aerosols; engineering controls such as HEPA filtration reduce airborne infectious particles when source control is needed in clinical settings.

If the RPD rest fits the cast but not the mouth, the cast does not represent the patient (impression/cast error). Remake the final impression and framework; redoing on the same cast repeats the error.
Bonding near a recent surgical site fails mainly because of blood, saliva, and poor isolation—moisture contamination prevents reliable adhesive polymerisation more than “wrong wire” alone.

Oral bilateral white striae with atrophy plus genital ulcers and wrist lesions is lichen planus; topical corticosteroid (triamcinolone acetonide) is first-line local therapy, not antivirals or nystatin alone.

When margins are sealed but the composite is undercontoured with open contact, conservative proximal repair (refresh/repair the box and rebuild contact) is preferred over full replacement if the remainder is sound.
Syphilis (positive venereal serology with oral ulcer) is treated with penicillin as the drug of choice; acyclovir treats herpes, not Treponema pallidum.

About 25% bone loss with imperfect glycemic control (HbA1c ~8%) typically maps to a fair periodontal prognosis—worse than good but not automatically hopeless/questionable without other severe factors.
Maxillary first premolar furcation has a short root trunk and deep mesial developmental concavity, giving the worst furcation access/prognosis among common teeth listed.

Paroxysmal hemicrania (and related indomethacin-responsive trigeminal autonomic cephalalgias) is diagnosed/treated by dramatic response to indomethacin; oxygen is the classic acute therapy for cluster headache.

If an asthmatic’s own bronchodilator fails and breathing remains difficult, activate EMS while giving oxygen and further rescue bronchodilator/epinephrine per emergency protocol—do not only repeat ineffective self-care.
Radiating left arm and jaw pain in an obese diabetic suggests acute coronary syndrome; glyceryl trinitrate (nitroglycerin) is the classic anti-anginal given (with aspirin) when blood pressure allows.

Severe pain within ~24 hours after instrumentation of a necrotic tooth with apical pathology is a classic endodontic flare-up from extruded debris/inflammation, not necessarily a new crack or wrong tooth.

For apical surgery on a molar with a large lesion, a sulcular (intrasulcular) flap with vertical releasing incision gives wide access and visibility; semilunar flaps are limited and risk scarring/limited exposure.
An asymptomatic dome-shaped antral soft-tissue dome over a tooth (antral pseudocyst/mucous retention phenomenon) usually needs no treatment beyond observation if the tooth is sound.
MRSA transmits primarily by direct contact with colonized skin/wounds or contaminated hands and fomites, not classic airborne droplet nuclei like measles.

Among usable options, influenza transmission involves close respiratory exposure; droplet/contact precautions apply. (True primary mode is respiratory droplets/aerosols rather than blood-borne contact alone.)
A 16-year-old seeking abortion-related care is outside routine dental scope and often legally/ethically complex; appropriate action is timely referral to a suitable medical facility/service.
Prophy cups are single-use disposable devices; after use they are discarded, not cleaned for reuse on another patient.
Burning tongue with a dry-mouth complaint but clinically moist mucosa is classic burning mouth syndrome (dysesthesia), not primary xerostomia from salivary hypofunction alone.
Cervical enamel projections into the furcation promote plaque retention and attachment loss; management includes removing/ODontoplasty of the enamel projection with periodontal therapy.
Bleeding on probing reflects the severity of gingival inflammation (ulcerated pocket epithelium and vascularity), not merely the anatomic location of the inflammation.
Third-trimester irreversible pulpitis still needs definitive pulp therapy; perform RCT with essential diagnostic/working radiographs and shielding rather than delaying until after delivery.
Piezoelectric scalers vibrate in a primarily linear back-and-forth pattern; magnetostrictive units have a more elliptical/orbital tip motion.
Digoxin toxicity and adverse effects include gastrointestinal symptoms and can increase gagging/nausea; among listed cardiac drugs it is the agent classically linked to gag/nausea complaints.
Primate spaces (mesial to upper canines, distal to lower canines) help accommodate permanent incisors and resolve mild crowding during the transition of the dentition.
With aging, recession, or progressive periodontitis, interdental papillae may flatten and disappear, creating black triangles rather than retaining pointed papillary form.
Shallow superficial tongue lacerations often heal well by secondary intention without layered suturing; deep through-and-through wounds need layered closure.

Conventional GIC remains moisture-sensitive early; finishing is limited after initial set, with definitive polishing delayed (commonly next visit/~24 h). Among timed options, early minimal finish after set is the listed choice.

Uncontrolled diabetes predisposes to severe soft-tissue infection and rapid fascial spread; necrotizing fasciitis is a life-threatening polymicrobial process that can present with limb necrosis/gangrene.
